The model was evaluated based on the benchmark data and enterprise observability space simulation. The results show that it had high level of performance with a lower Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) by 63.6 percent, lower Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR) by 60 percent and higher detecting accuracy by 16 percent and reduction of false-positive rates by 72 percent compared to traditional monitoring. These results suggest that the possibility of the framework can be based on the provision of proactive information, the accelerated recovery of the incident and less alert fatigue. Recent progress in smart incident management systems development could become the future of the study itself regarding explainable AI models, multicloud architecture, and self-healing observability pipeline.

title: Design and Analysis of a Wideband Inverse Dual C-Slot Microstrip Antenna for 5G, WiMAX, WLAN, and Wi-Fi 6E Bands
authors: Ratnesh Sagar, Suryansh Pratap Singh Rajawat, Hemant Choubey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A novel, compact microstrip patch antenna is introduced, featuring a dual C-slotted radiator and a Defected Ground Structure (DGS) to optimize impedance bandwidth and matching. With a miniature form factor of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28 \times 26 \times 1.6 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex> on an FR-4 substrate, the design supports a wide impedance bandwidth from 2.8 GHz to 8.3 GHz. Simulation data confirms excellent dual-band performance, with deep nulls in the reflection coefficient at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.8 \text{GHz}(-24.3 ~\text{dB})$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.2 \text{GHz}(-49$</tex> dB). This makes the antenna a viable candidate for multiple services, including 5G NR bands n77/n78/n79, WiMAX, WLAN, and the new Wi-Fi 6E spectrum. Additional performance metrics include a maximum gain of 3.4 dBi, consistent 50Ω input impedance, and a VSWR maintained below 2.0 across the entire operating band.

title: CDAF-Net: Fusing Unpaired and Imbalanced Multi-Modal Data for Ocular Disease Classification
authors: Renu Fathima A J, Aafiathul Zuleha M, Ribu Hasini A, Mathivanan N
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Deep learning models for medical tomography have shown great, really, promise but are often limited by the available data. Sometimes, while multiple imaging modalities like fundus photography and OCT are used for diagnosis, world datasets for these are typically unmatched, disjoint. What's more, severely imbalanced in size, in ophthalmology. This origin unbalance presents a critical, pretty much, barrier to developing reliable multimodal AI. Think about it this way: look, but here's what's interest: this paper introduces a novel two-part solution to this job. Let me put it this way: besides, clearly, naturally, first, we propose a Strategic Subset Creation algorithm, a data-centric methodology to curate a perfectly class-balanced, mixed-modality dataset from multiple disparate sources. Honestly, but here's what's interesting: here' s why this matters: second, we advise the Cross-Domain Attention Fusion, quite, Network (CDAF-Net), a dual-encoder architecture featuring a novel Gated Coalition mechanism. Indeed, the truth is: definitely, this block explicitly learns to integrate features from the unpaired domains, forcing the model to develop a modality-invariant understanding of disease. Surprisingly, using two public datasets, our approach achieves a high overall truth of 88.7% and a macro AUC of 0.962 on a 5 -class classification task. Let me put it this way: truth is, no doubt, you know, in fact, our preliminary results, including t-SNE visualizations, prove that our method successfully solves the source imbalance problem and acquires a unified feature space, presenting a viable path for training on real-world, imperfect clinical datum.

title: Predicting Customer Churn in Streaming Services Using Decision Tree Algorithms
authors: Monica Luthra, Shubham Sourav, Dwarkesh Mishra, Yash Verma, Mayank Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Customer churn has emerged as a dominant concern to streaming services that operate through subscriptions because it is sometimes less expensive to retain users rather than to attract them. Service providers can understand which clients are at risk and take proactive retention measures with the help of accurate prediction of the churn. The current paper proposes a churn prediction model that is implemented using decision tree algorithms to analyze activity patterns of customers and categorize subscribers as churn and non-churn. The research involves historical streaming data (demographics, subscription, and engagement measures) to train and test the model. The choice of decision trees is based on interpretability, efficiency in computation, and capability of working with heterogeneous data. The proposed model shows a good level of predictive performance, as it provides essential factors determining customer attrition including the frequency of viewing, 1, 3, and 6 months subscription, and the history of payments. The results justify the use of data in making decisions in customer relationship management (CRM) because they provide useful insights on how to decrease the rate of attrition and increase the lifetime value of clients. They can be further integrated with deep learning and ensemble models in future studies to be able to achieve better results in terms of accuracy and generalization.

title: State of Charge Distribution Estimation in Lithium-ion Battery Using Ultrasonic Scanning and GWO-MLP Model
authors: Ting Wang, Youyuan Wang, Dongning Huang, Zhiwei Shen
publish: 2025 3rd Power Electronics and Power System Conference (PEPSC)
abstract: The development of electric vehicles and large-scale energy storage systems is advancing rapidly. This has made accurate monitoring of lithium-ion battery SOC a key technological requirement for BMS. Traditional SOC estimation techniques primarily depend on extrinsic parameters such as voltage and current, which are challenging to mirror the spatial heterogeneity within the battery and may inducing local performance deterioration and safety concerns. In this paper, a nondestructive SOC spatial distribution estimation method is proposed. This method is based on ultrasonic transmission scanning and a Gray Wolf Optimized Multi-Layer Perceptron (GWO-MLP) model, which achieves an accuracy of 3.88% RMSE,2.19% MAE, and 0.985 R2 in SOC estimation of a commercial lithium-ion pouch battery. Meanwhile, a successful reconstruction of the sub millimeter resolution SOC spatial distribution map has been achieved, which clearly reveals the existence of non-uniform phenomena inside the cell and localized deviation. This study demonstrates the significant potential of integrating ultrasonic detection with deep learning for the nondestructive testing of batteries.

title: Environment-Aware Intention Generation in Robots via Multistage Perception and Language Reasoning
authors: Shakil Arifeen, Ometh Perera, S. M. U. S. Samarakoon, C. N. Perera, H. M. K. K. M. B. Herath, S. L. P. Yasakethu
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents a real-time framework for generating humanoid intent designed for service-oriented robots operating across diverse environments. The system integrates visual perception, environment classification, and a hybrid large language model (LLM) reasoning to enable context-aware, human-like behavior. A YOLOv8x-based perception module detects 43 object classes with a mAP@0.5 of 0.89. In contrast, object distributions are processed by a lightweight artificial neural network (ANN) that classifies environments with high accuracy (≈99.4%) and near-perfect F1-scores across all classes. A hybrid LLM mechanism governs reasoning. When ANN confidence is high, Groq’s Llama 3.3 70B model provides rapid, low-latency (~36.3ms) intent generation; when confidence is low, OpenAI GPT-4o is invoked for vision-based inference and richer semantic reasoning. The framework operates on a cloud-edge deployment, with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) handling inference and a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B capturing live video streams. A Flask-based web interface visualizes object detections, environment predictions, and generates intentions with real-time audio-visual feedback. Experimental results demonstrate the system’s ability to maintain real-time performance while producing contextually appropriate, socially aligned responses, establishing its potential for adaptive and assistive humanoid robotic applications.

title: Multi-Task Hybrid Deep Learning with Cross-Domain Alignment for Real-Time Sentiment Analysis in Clinical Decision Support Systems
authors: Habibor R Rabby, Md Habibul Arif, Md Iftekhar Monzur Tanvir, Nusrat Yasmin Nadia, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in healthcare enables early detection of mental health risks, assessment of patient satisfaction, and support for clinical decision-making. However, existing approaches often address medical sentiment classification and mental health status detection as separate tasks, limiting their ability to leverage shared linguistic cues and adapt across heterogeneous data sources. This paper presents a Multi-Task Hybrid Deep Learning with Cross-Domain Alignment framework for realtime sentiment analysis in clinical decision support systems. The proposed architecture integrates a shared transformer-based encoder, a domain alignment module, and task-specific classification heads to jointly model two complementary datasets: a multi-class mental health status corpus and a medical sentiment dataset. Experiments on both datasets demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in accuracy, macro-F1, and other evaluation metrics while maintaining sub- 50 ms inference latency suitable for real-time deployment. These results highlight the potential of cross-domain multi-task learning to improve sentiment analysis performance in diverse healthcare contexts, enabling more responsive and context-aware clinical decision support.

title: Enhancing Building Change Detection in ChangeFormer by Integrating Deformable Attention Transformer
authors: Seung Bae Jeon, Myeong-Hun Jeong
abstract: Urban building change detection in remote sensing imagery supports planning, disaster response, and environmental monitoring; however, dense layouts, diverse building types, and varying imaging conditions still challenge existing models. We propose deformable attention transformer for enhanced building change detection (DA-ChangeFormer), which integrates a deformable attention transformer (DAT) into ChangeFormer. DAT adaptively focuses on regions with high change likelihood and enables flexible feature sampling, capturing subtle changes more effectively than fixed self-attention while suppressing pseudo-changes from illumination or seasonal shifts. We evaluate the proposed method on the LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and DSIFN-CD datasets. On LEVIR-CD, DA-ChangeFormer achieves 92.8% F1 and 87.3% IoU, outperforming our reimplementation of ChangeFormer by 1.06 and 1.62 points, respectively, and the originally reported ChangeFormer results by 2.40 and 4.80 points. Across datasets, the model demonstrates strong generalization in complex urban environments. In addition to performance gains, the proposed architecture reduces the parameter count relative to ChangeFormer under matched model configurations and shows modest FLOPs savings in the 256×256 setting while maintaining comparable FLOPs in the 512×512 setting. Overall, DA-ChangeFormer demonstrates that selectively integrating deformable attention into semantically rich encoder stages improves localization accuracy while maintaining competitive computational cost.

title: An Analysis of the Human Ability to Detect Deepfakes With Geopolitical Content
authors: Michele Brienza, Marta Golotta, Marco Romano, Daniele Nardi, Domenico D. Bloisi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The increasing diffusion of deepfakes has raised significant global concerns, especially due to their potential geopolitical implications. This concern relates to the spread of false information that can mislead people and have a serious impact on societies. However, identifying what can misinform people is not trivial. In this work, we present an experimental study that involves a sample of students of different backgrounds. Three different political deepfakes were created and shown to them. The perceived values were then analyzed using a specific questionnaire. The experimental results show significant differences in the ability to discern the authenticity of the proposed videos depending on the level of awareness of the contents viewed. This demonstrates the crucial role of education on deepfakes in countering the spread of misinformation.

title: Few-Shot Medical Image Segmentation With Hierarchical Hypercorrelation Vision State-Space Model
authors: Zheng Cao, Bang Du, Danqing Hu, Wei Zhou, Shangde Gao, Rong Tan, Jun Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Few-shot medical image segmentation (FSMS) seeks to delineate novel anatomical structures using limited annotated examples, thereby reducing dependence on extensive manual labelling. Conventional methods face challenges with computational inefficiency, overfitting, and information loss, particularly in high-ambiguity medical contexts. To overcome these limitations, we propose the hierarchical hypercorrelation vision state-space model (H2VSS). The novelty of H2VSS is twofold. First, we introduce a dedicated HyperCorrelation Block (HCB) designed to address the low-contrast and contextual ambiguity endemic to medical imaging. This module computes and fuses dense correlations derived simultaneously from both target-level (foreground-focused) and image-level (context-aware) support features, providing a robust feature basis that methods relying on target features alone cannot achieve. Second, H2VSS presents a novel architectural synergy by integrating vision state-space (VSS) blocks into a hierarchical, multiscale feature matching pipeline. This design leverages the linear-complexity and long-range dependency modeling of VSS to enhance matching consistency beyond prior Transformer-based hierarchical methods, while remaining architecturally distinct from recent sequential Mamba-based FSMS models. Evaluated on three public medical image datasets, H2VSS demonstrates superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) FSMS models, outperforming the prior SOTA by a significant margin.

title: Lightweight Learning from Actuation-Space Demonstrations via Flow Matching for Whole-Body Soft Robotic Grasping
authors: Liudi Yang, Yang Bai, Yuhao Wang, Ibrahim Alsarraj, Gitta Kutyniok, Zhanchi Wang, Ke Wu
abstract: Robotic grasping under uncertainty remains a fundamental challenge due to its uncertain and contact-rich nature. Traditional rigid robotic hands, with limited degrees of freedom and compliance, rely on complex model-based and heavy feedback controllers to manage such interactions. Soft robots, by contrast, exhibit embodied mechanical intelligence: their underactuated structures and passive flexibility of their whole body naturally accommodate uncertain contacts and enable adaptive behaviors. To harness this capability, we propose a lightweight actuation-space learning framework that infers distributional control representations for whole-body soft robotic grasping directly from deterministic demonstrations using a flow matching model (Rectified Flow), without requiring dense sensing or heavy control loops. Trained with only 30 demonstrations covering less than 8% of the reachable workspace, the learned policy achieved a 97.5% grasp success rate over 1000 trials in simulation. In real-world experiments on 50 uniformly distributed targets, the policy achieved a 100% success rate, generalized to object size variations from -33% to +100%, and remained stable under execution-time scaling from 20% to 200%. These results demonstrate that actuation-space learning effectively embeds mechanical intelligence into control, significantly reducing reliance on centralized computation for grasping under uncertainty.

title: A Planning-Oriented Autonomous Driving Framework: From Image to Trajectory With Intent-Aware Prediction
authors: Hong-Yi Kang, Jun-Guo Lu, Ming Wei, Qing-Hao Zhang, Zhen Zhu, YaFei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Autonomous driving requires accurate prediction of surrounding agents and reliable planning of ego-vehicle trajectories, yet integrating these components into a coherent pipeline remains challenging. This paper proposes a planning-oriented framework that maps bird’s-eye-view images directly into executable ego-vehicle trajectories, balancing an end-to-end formulation with modular components. The system operates in three sequential stages: multi-agent intent-aware prediction, where intent embeddings are injected into agent encodings together with static environment structures to enhance joint feature fusion, followed by a trajectory decoding stage that further exploits intent to guide sequential prediction; goal-conditioned trajectory generation via an imitation-learning planner with cross-attention and a transformer decoder; and geometric post-processing to enforce obstacle clearance and trajectory smoothness. Throughout the framework, road structure features can be obtained from coarse semantic layers in the bird’s-eye-view domain, which provides sufficient abstraction for prediction and planning while preserving flexibility and avoiding reliance on high-definition maps. Evaluations on the nuPlan benchmark show that the proposed method achieves strong and well-balanced performance across standard non-reactive and reactive planning metrics, while maintaining real-time efficiency with an average overall pipeline latency of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$164{\,}\mathrm {ms}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. In addition, a preliminary deployment on an Intelligent Guided Vehicle in a mixed-traffic container terminal demonstrates the practical deployability of the proposed framework in real-world industrial scenarios. Although not fully differentiable, the design intentionally balances end-to-end learning with structural modularity to ensure interpretability, flexibility, and robustness, ensuring both practical feasibility and strong closed-loop performance.

title: A Simulation Platform for MARL Training and Evaluation in Swarm Confrontation_supp1-3677714.pdf
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: In swarm confrontation, robots must swiftly formulate strategies in transient environments, a challenge well-suited for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL). However, existing platforms suffer from the lack of comprehensive confrontation scenario modeling and scalable frameworks, hindering MARL's widespread applications. We introduce a novel platform for training, simulating, and evaluating MARL algorithms in swarm confrontation tasks. It constructs a holistic simulation framework by integrating robot, environment, and rule models for complex confrontation scenarios. Equipped with a decentralized task allocator and path planner for each robot, the platform enables scalable cooperation across dynamic environments. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our platform simulates confrontations involving up to twenty agents per side, providing empirical guidance for algorithm selection in various settings.

title: RISCAM: A 576-Element 2-D Scalable Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface With Multibeam RF Camera at X-Band for ISAC
authors: Tyler James Blundo, Zijian Shao, Juho Park, Emir Ali Karahan, Alex Stepko, Kaushik Sengupta
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) mitigate non-line-of-sight (NLOS) challenges but remain limited in 5G wireless systems due to poor scalability and the absence of integrated user localization needed for low-latency beam management and joint sensing–communication. Conventional AP-driven beam management adds significant delay and signaling overhead. This work introduces the first RIS with built-in user localization using a shared aperture integrating an RF camera forming 13 simultaneous beams. The 576-element (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$24\times 24$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) surface forms reconfigurable beams scanning –50° to 50°, achieving ~36 dB directivity and ~3° beamwidth. Thirteen sensing subarrays (208 elements) enable dual-purpose sensing and reflection, realizing an RF camera with <2° RMSE localization accuracy. By combining multibeam sensing with reconfigurable reflection, the system can estimate the angle of arrival (AoA) and dynamically retro-reflect or steer wavefronts. Measurements validate precise AoA estimation, effective beam steering, and strong agreement with full-wave simulations for future 6G integrated sensing and communications (ISACs).

title: A Novel Vector Control Integrated for Grid-Connected Kite-Based Airborne Wind Energy Conversion
authors: Pankaj Kumar, Yashwant Kashyap, K. Manjunatha Sharma
publish: IEEE Canadian Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering
abstract: Kite-based airborne wind energy conversion system (KAWECS) provides an innovative and interesting concept to exploit high-altitude winds for efficient power generation. The framework of this system utilizes a flying kite connected to the generator through a power electronics interface for grid integration. However, maintaining stable generator speed and maximum power extraction is very challenging due to the dynamic and periodic nature of the kite motion. This study presents a novel vector control integrated to enhance the performance of a grid-connected kite-based airborne wind energy conversion. The vector control scheme is designed to control the speed and torque of the permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG), ensuring optimal power extraction under varying wind conditions. A novel optimal symmetrical method is used to design the controller gain of the vector control scheme. Furthermore, a grid-side converter (GSC) controller is designed to regulate the DC-link voltage and synchronize the current and voltage with the grid. The LCL filter is designed to improve the quality of the grid voltage and current. The proposed system is implemented in MATLAB Simulink and validated hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) using a field programmable gate array (FPGA)-based OPAL-RT OP-4520 platform. The results demonstrated stable generator speed tracking, regulated DC-link voltage at 800 V, and distortion-free grid currents with a total harmonic distortion (THD) of 5.95%. Furthermore, the proposed control strategy improved system performance by achieving a 45.6% capacity factor, compared to 31.4% for the conventional wind turbine system (CWTS). These quantitative results confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed controller for real-time grid-connected airborne wind energy applications.

title: Toward Generalizable Forgery Detection and Reasoning
authors: Yueying Gao, Dongliang Chang, Bingyao Yu, Haotian Qin, Muxi Diao, Lei Chen, Kongming Liang, Zhanyu Ma
abstract: Accurate and interpretable detection of AI-generated images is essential for mitigating risks associated with AI misuse. However, the substantial domain gap among generative models makes it challenging to develop a generalizable forgery detection model. Moreover, since every pixel in an AI-generated image is synthesized, traditional saliency-based forgery explanation methods are not well suited for this task. To address these challenges, we formulate detection and explanation as a unified Forgery Detection and Reasoning task (FDR-Task)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>, leveraging Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to provide accurate detection through reliable reasoning over forgery attributes. To facilitate this task, we introduce the Multi-Modal Forgery Reasoning dataset (MMFR-Dataset), a large-scale dataset containing 120K images across 10 generative models, with 378K reasoning annotations on forgery attributes, enabling comprehensive evaluation of the FDR-Task. Furthermore, we propose FakeReasoning, a forgery detection and reasoning framework with three key components: 1) a dual-branch visual encoder that integrates CLIP and DINO to capture both high-level semantics and low-level artifacts; 2) a Forgery-Aware Feature Fusion Module that leverages DINO’s attention attention maps and cross-attention mechanisms to guide MLLMs toward forgery-related clues; 3) a Classification Probability Mapper that couples language modeling and forgery detection, enhancing overall performance. Experiments across multiple generative models demonstrate that FakeReasoning not only achieves robust generalization but also outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both detection and reasoning tasks.

title: Dynamic Impedance-Aligned Phase Angle Compensation for Targeted Harmonic Mitigation in PMSM Drives
authors: Armita Fatemimoghadam, Caniggia Viana, Lakshmi Varaha Iyer, Narayan C. Kar
abstract: This article introduces a dynamic impedance-aligned phase-angle compensation (DIA-PAC) for the targeted harmonic mitigation (PACTHM) control method, which is designed to enhance the performance of interior permanent-magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM) drives by effectively suppressing critical $\textbf{6}\boldsymbol{n} \pm \textbf{1}$ harmonics, including the 5th, 7th, 11th, and 13th orders. Unlike triplen harmonics, which cancel out in balanced systems, $\textbf{6}\boldsymbol{n} \pm \textbf{1}$ harmonics significantly degrade motor performance, leading to increased torque ripple, thermal stress, and reduced efficiency. The proposed approach incorporates dynamic PAC blocks that counteract load-impedance-angle disturbances in real time, ensuring reliable harmonic mitigation under varying operating conditions. The methodology also leverages a multiple dq-axis framework to selectively eliminate harmonics, an expansion of standard field-oriented control (FOC) that preserves the baseline loop and thus makes the method scalable in the number of targeted harmonics. Experimental results on a 22-kW IPMSM prototype with a voltage-source-inverter (VSI) setup show substantial reductions in total harmonic distortion (THD) and torque ripple. Using a rapid-control-prototyping (RCP) testbed, the findings show that the PACTHM method achieves superior harmonic suppression and improved system power quality, making it a practical solution for industrial PMSM applications.

title: A Novel Piezoelectric-Driven Small Bionic Underwater Robot_supp3-3672852.mp4
authors: Liang Wang
abstract: In deep-sea exploration, compact robotic systems encounter considerable difficulties in sustaining effective propulsion under tremendous hydrostatic pressure exceeding 100 MPa. Conventional electric drive systems suffer significant energy losses due to the requirement for large pressure-resistant enclosures, whereas current actuators utilizing functional materials frequently display constraints such as low thrust-to-weight ratios, elevated drive voltages, or insufficient pressure resistance. This work produced a unique seal-free small bionic underwater robot (SBUR) powered by piezoelectric actuation, inspired by anguilliform locomotion. A dual-SBUR system was employed to enhance the design of an underwater mobile platform (UMP), facilitating combined forward propulsion and steering capabilities. First, the structure and design of the SBUR and UMP are elucidated. Subsequently, modal features in air were examined by the transfer matrix method (TMM), while hydrodynamic responses in water were explored utilizing computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations. Performance evaluations conducted under standard pressure settings revealed a consistent SBUR forward speed of 152 mm/s. The UMP achieved a forward speed of 43.5 mm/s, generated a thrust of 20 mN, and performed rotations with a minimum radius of 130 mm. Experiments validated the practical feasibility of the SBUR at 110 MPa. The performance decline under elevated hydrostatic pressure did not surpass 3.3%, and it demonstrated negligible temperature sensitivity. Practical validation confirmed the SBUR’s ability to transport payloads of up to 10 kg, highlighting its suitability for deep-sea applications. The proposed robot offers advantages such as miniaturization, low operating voltage, and excellent adaptability to extreme hydrostatic pressure.

title: Rapid Assessment of Earthquake-Induced Building Damage Using Optical Image Correlation
authors: Chenglong Li, Yanxing Wu, Xinjian Shan, Zhangfeng Ma, Xiaogang Song, Guohong Zhang, Shengji Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Rapid assessment of earthquake-damaged building-such as area, quantity, and severity-is crucial for timely post-earthquake rescue. Pre- and post-earthquake data collected from optical remote sensing systems can effectively resolve earthquake deformation. Among them, the normalized correlation coefficient reduction (NCCR) shows promise in directly reflecting building damage. Here we propose an automated processing chain for building damage detection, which integrates subpixel image correlation technique, deep-learning-based segmentation and anomaly detection models, and a weight stacking method. We applied this chain to assess building damage of 77 cities affected by the 2023 Türkiye earthquake doublet. Our results demonstrate that the method effectively and accurately detects earthquake-induced building damages, aligning closely with officially reported damage and PGA data. By comparing our findings with official damage degree data from Kahramanmaraş, we constrained the NCCR thresholds for five damage levels: no damage (0.21), slight damage (0.37), moderate damage (0.50), serious damage (0.71), and destroyed (0.82). Additionally, we found that cities on the fault hanging wall and footwall need to be over 23 km and 12 km away from the fault, respectively, to experience low damage levels (NCCR below 0.16). This reveals significant differences in building damage between the two fault walls, improving our understanding of the relationship between building damage and proximity to causative faults. Our proposed methodology holds great potential for rapidly providing critical building damage information, thereby supporting efficient post-earthquake rescue and emergency response efforts.
